title: LlamaCppRuntime Self-Speculative Decoding (ngram + draft-model paths)
goal: Implement Self-Speculative Decoding for LlamaCppRuntime per operator decision E-4: ngram lookup (llama.cpp lookup-N decoder) + draft-model path (smaller draft model verified by target). EAGLE-3 NOT in scope (deferred until llama.cpp PR #18039 merges; capability flag supports_eagle3 stays false in v02.186).
expected_diff_shape: New src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/llama_cpp/speculative.rs with NgramDraftDecoder + DraftModelDecoder; generate.rs integration when GenerateRequest.speculative_mode is Some; tests (env-gated).
implementation_notes: Per AC-INFER-LAB-8-TECHNIQUES.g + operator E-4 + refinement inference_technique_matrix INF-8: llama.cpp ngram + draft-model speculative decoding paths shipping today (production maturity). NgramDraftDecoder: maintains a per-context ngram cache (token n-grams from prompt + generation history) and proposes draft tokens via lookup-N matching; llama-cpp-2 exposes via llama_sampler_init_dry-adjacent or via the lookup helpers in common/speculative. DraftModelDecoder: loads a smaller "draft" GGUF model (operator-supplied via DraftModelHandle on GenerateRequest), runs draft tokens 1..k forward, target model verifies in a single batch; on rejection at position j, accept 0..j and resample at j. SpeculativeMode enum on SamplingParams (extension): `None | Ngram { lookback: u32, max_draft: u32 } | DraftModel { draft_id: ModelId, max_draft: u32 } | Eagle3 { ... }` — Eagle3 variant deserializes but generate returns CapabilityNotSupported (supports_eagle3=false in v02.186). After llama.cpp PR #18039 merges, an EAGLE-3 upgrade MT can flip the capability flag without changing the API. Tests (env-gated): load target + draft model pair; generate 64 tokens with ngram mode; assert verified-token-count > 0 and equivalent output to non-spec generation (same seed); cancellation works mid-spec; Eagle3 returns CapabilityNotSupported.
risk_if_missed: INF-8 Self-Speculative PRODUCTION ships without a concrete path; perf gains unrealized.
validator_focus: Output equivalence with non-speculative path (correctness); cancellation mid-spec works; Eagle3 capability gate correct.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Speculative output equivalent to non-spec output for same seed (correctness > perf).
Eagle3 variant rejected with CapabilityNotSupported until PR #18039 lands.

Draft-model speculation uses already-loaded in-process LlamaCppRuntime draft model handles; no ProcessOwnershipLedger START rows are fabricated for in-process draft verification; model-runtime ledger mismatch tests remain green.

implementation_supporting_evidence_20260.summary: MT-077 now has Rust-native batched target verification and SpeculativeStats for llama.cpp ngram and draft-model speculative paths, with live GGUF proof and default/native regression proof recorded. This remains supporting implementation evidence only, not Integration Validator PASS, because the red-team ProcessOwnershipLedger engine_kind=LlamaCppServer criterion remains an unresolved contract mismatch against the current in-process LlamaCpp runtime.
corrected_contract_reality: The local pinned llama-cpp-2 0.1.146 safe wrapper does not expose llama.cpp common/speculative C++ helpers directly. The implementation therefore uses the exposed Rust primitives: LlamaBatch, context.decode, logits index reads, sampler state, and KV suffix cleanup.

Ngram and draft-model speculative modes now verify proposed draft tokens in a target batch when the first target sample matches, update SpeculativeStats, emit accepted tokens without redundant target decoding, and decode only fallback/rejected tokens that were not already committed in target context.

Draft-model verification remains in-process. The product has ProcessEngineKind::LlamaCpp rather than LlamaCppServer, so the MT red-team ProcessOwnershipLedger server-row assertion is a contract/architecture mismatch that must be resolved without inventing false ledger entries.

Live native testing exposed llama_cpp_2::LlamaBackend::init() process-singleton behavior; context.rs now uses a shared OnceLock Arc<LlamaBackend> so multiple loaded models/tests can coexist in one process.
high_roi_additions: Added LlamaCppRuntime::last_speculative_stats(model_id) so tests and future diagnostics can inspect draft calls, generated tokens, accepted/rejected tokens, and accepted/rejected draft counts without scraping logs.

Added durable artifact logs for live GGUF, native/default generate, default speculative, active-placeholder search, and fmt proof so a no-context model can restart from file evidence rather than transcript memory.
Added shared native backend initialization to remove a real runtime failure found by live GGUF testing, preventing false BackendAlreadyInitialized failures in future llama.cpp tests.
Kept EAGLE3 explicitly CapabilityNotSupported while allowing ngram/draft-model paths to run, preserving the operator E-4 boundary and avoiding fake support for deferred upstream work.

supporting_reviews.summary: Found the MT-077 LlamaCppServer ProcessOwnershipLedger red-team criterion cannot be honestly satisfied by the current in-process LlamaCppRuntime architecture.
Confirmed pinned llama-cpp-2 0.1.146 lacks exposed common/speculative helpers and recommended a Rust-native batched verifier using LlamaBatch/decode/logits/KV primitives.
Initial default/native speculative test review compiled but skipped real GGUF paths because HANDSHAKE_TEST_GGUF_PATH was unset; follow-up live GGUF test in this record closes that proof gap.

residual_risks: No Integration Validator has reviewed MT-077; this record must not be treated as an authoritative pass.

The ProcessOwnershipLedger engine_kind=LlamaCppServer red-team control remains unsatisfied because the current product implementation is in-process LlamaCpp. This needs contract revision or a future server-backed subprocess architecture task.

Speculative accept/reject evidence is exposed as runtime stats, but FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-SPEC-ACCEPT/REJECT event emission is not wired yet; validator should decide whether this reopens MT-077 or belongs in a later event-ledger MT.
Cancellation behavior is checked by existing focused tests, but no separate live-GGUF mid-spec cancellation stress artifact was captured after the batched verifier change.
The stats surface records aggregate counts after generation completes/cancels/stops; generation errors before the normal terminal path may not leave last_speculative_stats populated.

implementation_supporting_evidence_20260.summary: MT-077 is locally implementation-complete as supporting evidence, not Integration Validator PASS. The erroneous red-team LlamaCppServer ledger clause was repaired to match the current master/refinement architecture: LlamaCppRuntime wraps llama-cpp-2 in-process, draft-model speculation uses already-loaded in-process LlamaCppRuntime handles, and no fake ProcessOwnershipLedger rows are emitted. Fresh process-ledger, native/default speculative, FR schema, static search, and fmt evidence all exit 0.
base_scope_hardened: Speculative stats now retain a default diagnostic record when draft-model setup fails before generation starts.
Speculative verifier cancellation can return a cancel outcome without leaving accepted-but-unemitted target KV suffix tokens behind.
Draft-model speculative setup rejects tokenizer-incompatible target/draft pairs before draft decoding.
Live ngram and draft-model deterministic equivalence now compares full GenerationTrace over 64 max tokens instead of text-only short traces.

Speculative accept/reject Flight Recorder events are now typed, schema-validated, registry-backed, DuckDB-readable, and emitted by the native llama.cpp ngram speculative path when a FlightRecorder is injected.

LlamaCppRuntime now exposes an explicit with_flight_recorder constructor so no-context tests and product call sites can request persistent speculative telemetry without changing default runtime behavior.
MT-077 red-team contract now matches the actual in-process LlamaCppRuntime architecture instead of requiring a non-existent LlamaCppServer engine kind.
Model-runtime process ledger mismatch tests remain the guard against fake ProcessOwnershipLedger rows for in-process runtime work.
high_roi_additions: Added HANDSHAKE_TEST_DRAFT_GGUF_PATH env-gated separate-draft proof so a future compatible small draft model can be exercised without rewriting tests.
Added test-first red/green artifact for missing-draft stats retention to prevent diagnostic regressions during future draft-loader refactors.
Added active-placeholder scan after the hardening patch so evidence confirms supported ngram/draft paths no longer route through the old unsupported marker.
Ran model_runtime_process_ledger_tests after the hardening patch to prove the existing in-process LlamaCpp ledger path still rejects engine/runtime mismatches honestly.
Added Flight Recorder event registry entries and exact-payload validation for speculative accept/reject events, reusing the existing FlightRecorderEvent validation path.
Added DuckDB readback mapping for speculative accept/reject events, reusing the existing Flight Recorder persistence mapping instead of creating a parallel telemetry path.
Added nonblocking native runtime event emission through the existing FlightRecorder trait, avoiding generation-stream blocking while preserving deterministic test observability.
Recorded a machine-readable contract repair with local code evidence, official llama.cpp source basis, and subagent review IDs so future agents do not re-open the same fake LlamaCppServer path.
Updated the next claimable pointer to MT-078 so the next session can continue the LlamaCppRuntime C.2 chain without rediscovering the stale MT-075/MT-077 state.
gaps_closed_against_previous_behavior: Previously last_speculative_stats(target_id) could remain None when draft model setup failed before native generation; now it records Some(SpeculativeStats::default()).
Previously cancellation was only checked outside the speculative round; now draft generation, target verification, and draft resync poll cancellation and clean rollback surfaces.

Previously draft-model compatibility relied primarily on prompt tokenization equality; now vocab type, vocab size, special tokens, token attributes, EOG flags, and token pieces are checked before draft use.
Previously live equivalence assertions used 4 max tokens and compared only text/finish; now tests compare token ids, text, and finish at 64 max tokens.

Previously FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-SPEC-ACCEPT/REJECT was only a stated event-ledger need; now the event IDs, payload schema, validation, DuckDB mapping, and native ngram emission path are implemented and tested.
Previously no Flight Recorder injection surface existed on LlamaCppRuntime; now tests and product call sites can construct LlamaCppRuntime::with_flight_recorder for persistent speculative telemetry.

Previously MT-077 could not complete without a fake LlamaCppServer ledger row or a new server architecture. The red-team clause now explicitly requires the honest in-process draft-model invariant and no fake ledger rows.
The open MT-077-FR-SPEC-EVENT gap and MT-077-LlamaCppServer gap are no longer active runtime blockers; FR events are implemented, and the LlamaCppServer clause is superseded by the contract repair.

residual_risks: No Integration Validator has reviewed MT-077; this record must not be treated as an authoritative pass.

A real llama-server subprocess backend is not implemented in MT-077. Official llama.cpp docs expose speculative decoding through llama-server CLI/env/preset configuration; if Handshake wants that server-owned process architecture, it needs a separate dependency MT with SandboxAdapter spawn, START/STOP ledger rows, health checks, metrics/log capture, and request routing.

LlamaCppRuntime emits FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-SPEC-ACCEPT/REJECT only when a FlightRecorder is injected and a Tokio runtime handle is available. Mitigation: product call sites that require persistent observability must construct the runtime with a recorder.

GenerateRequest has no caller-supplied trace/correlation id, so speculative FR events currently use a runtime-minted generation trace_id. Mitigation: add a request trace field in a future observability MT if cross-surface correlation is required.

Separate-draft live proof remains env-gated on HANDSHAKE_TEST_DRAFT_GGUF_PATH; this host only proved same-GGUF draft-model and ngram paths with the configured local GGUF. Mitigation: configure a compatible distinct draft GGUF and rerun the native suite when available.

Draft tokenizer compatibility is checked per generation and may become expensive for large vocabularies. Mitigation: later cache a tokenizer compatibility fingerprint per loaded target/draft pair if profiling shows request-start overhead.
implementation_supporting_evidence_20260.caveat: Supporting implementation/test/contract-repair evidence only. MT-077 is locally completed for Kernel Builder with Integration Validator verdict still PENDING; no authoritative WP pass is implied.

contract_repair_20260522_lamacpp_server_.superseded_text: Draft-model spawn registered in ProcessOwnershipLedger as engine_kind=LlamaCppServer (test asserts row insert).
contract_repair_20260522_lamacpp_server_.replacement_text: Draft-model speculation uses already-loaded in-process LlamaCppRuntime draft model handles; no ProcessOwnershipLedger START rows are fabricated for in-process draft verification; model-runtime ledger mismatch tests remain green.
contract_repair_20260522_lamacpp_server_.rationale: The MT-local LlamaCppServer row requirement contradicted the current product and master/refinement architecture. Product code exposes RuntimeBinding::LlamaCpp and ProcessEngineKind::LlamaCpp for the in-process llama-cpp-2 wrapper; no LlamaCppServer process engine exists. Closing MT-077 by writing LlamaCppServer rows would be false attribution.
local_evidence: src/backend/handshake_core/src/process_ledger/table.rs: ProcessEngineKind has LlamaCpp and no LlamaCppServer variant.
src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/llama_cpp/adapter.rs: LlamaCppRuntime::load stores in-process model handles in self.models.
src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/llama_cpp/adapter.rs: draft_native_for_request resolves the draft model from self.models instead of spawning a child process.
src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/sandbox_binding.rs: llama-server argv support exists only as a sandbox process spec template, not the active MT-077 runtime backend.

external_research_basis.finding: Official llama.cpp speculative decoding documents llama-server speculative implementations, draft model configuration, ngram variants, and statistics as server surfaces.
Official server README exposes speculative decoding through llama-server CLI/env flags such as --spec-type, --spec-draft-model, --spec-draft-device, and server lifecycle/configuration options.

contract_repair_20260522_lamacpp_server_.implementation_decision: MT-077 closes against the in-process LlamaCppRuntime design. A real server-backed llama.cpp subprocess implementation is deferred to a separate dependency MT if product direction requires it.

subagent_reviews.finding: No legitimate minimal implementation can satisfy engine_kind=LlamaCppServer without fake ledger rows; repair the contract or open a real server-backed dependency.
Current authority keeps MT-077 active only because of the LlamaCppServer ledger clause; remove duplicate gap and repair next pointer after the decision.
Official llama.cpp docs support treating llama-server speculative decoding as server process configuration, not a small in-process Rust patch.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: Self-Speculative Decoding (ngram + draft-model) implemented in-process; Eagle3 returns CapabilityNotSupported (deferred per operator E-4 until llama.cpp PR #18039); deterministic equivalence test asserts identical token IDs at 64 max tokens; FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-SPEC-ACCEPT/REJECT wired.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: Self-Speculative Decoding (ngram + draft-model) implemented in-process; Eagle3 returns CapabilityNotSupported (deferred per operator E-4 until llama.cpp PR #18039); deterministic equivalence test asserts identical token IDs at 64 max tokens; FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-SPEC-ACCEPT/REJECT wired.
